from dataclasses import dataclass
import re

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    """
    # Helper to clean quoted objects and strip extra whitespace
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        return o.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping from predicate to a template function that returns a clause.
    # The function receives (subject, object) and returns a string.
    predicate_templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": lambda s, o: f"The architectural style of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "buildingStartDate": lambda s, o: f"Construction of {s} began in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "completionDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was completed in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "floorCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean_obj(o)} floors.",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "cost": lambda s, o: f"The cost of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "floorArea": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers a floor area of {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "architect": lambda s, o: f"The architect {clean_obj(o)} designed {s}.",
        "owner": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "formerName": lambda s, o: f"{s} was formerly known as {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "height": lambda s, o: f"The height of {s} is {re.sub(r\"\\s*\\(.*\\)\", \"\", clean_obj(o)).strip()} metres.",
        "buildingType": lambda s, o: f"{s} is a {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "developer": lambda s, o: f"{s} was developed by {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "tenant": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a tenant: {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "currentTenants": lambda s, o: f"{s} currently houses {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "address": lambda s, o: f"The address of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "inaugurationDate": lambda s, o: f"The inauguration of {s} was on {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{clean_obj(o)} is the birthplace of {s}.",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{clean_obj(o)} is the death place of {s}.",
        "bedCount": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean_obj(o)} beds.",
        "region": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the region of {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "state": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the state of {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "website": lambda s, o: f"The website of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "yearOfConstruction": lambda s, o: f"{s} was constructed in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": lambda s, o: f"The NRHP reference number for {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": lambda s, o: f"{s} was added to the NRHP on {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "significantBuilding": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known for the building {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"The governing body of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{clean_obj(o)} is the leader of {s}.",
        "chancellor": lambda s, o: f"The chancellor of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "governmentType": lambda s, o: f"The government type of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"The capital of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The language of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"The leader title of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"The currency of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The ethnic group of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "significantProject": lambda s, o: f"{s} is known for the project {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "foundationPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded in {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "demonym": lambda s, o: f"{s} has demonym {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "numberOfRooms": lambda s, o: f"{s} has {clean_obj(o)} rooms.",
        "keyPerson": lambda s, o: f"{s} has key person {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "architecture": lambda s, o: f"{s} features architecture {clean_obj(o)}.",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"The postal code of {s} is {clean_obj(o)}.",
    }

    # Group triples by their subject.
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        parts = []
        for tr in group:
            template = predicate_templates.get(tr.predicate)
            if template:
                parts.append(template(subject, tr.object))
            else:
                # Fallback for unknown predicates.
                parts.append(f"{subject} {tr.predicate} {clean_obj(tr.object)}.")
        # Combine parts, ensuring proper punctuation.
        sentence = " ".join(parts).strip()
        sentences.append(sentence)

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
